preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap (or trill) to a voiceless palato-alveolar fricative occurs through a process of merger and assimilation, most famously observed in German dialects where clusters involving rhotics and sibilants shifted toward 'sh'.

examples.note: Historical shift where the cluster /rs/ merged into a single palato-alveolar fricative.
In many dialects (e.g. Lisbon, Rio), the coda rhotic or sibilant can be realized as a palato-alveolar fricative.

sources: Wiese, R. (1996). The Phonology of German.
Coutinho, I. L. (1976). Pontos de gramática histórica.
